title: crataegus × macrocarpa nothovar. bohemica
intro: Crataegus × macrocarpa nothovar. bohemica, also known as the Bohemian hawthorn, is a hybrid of the Crataegus monogyna and Crataegus laevigata species, which is a type of thorny shrub or small tree native to Central Europe. The Bohemian hawthorn has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and cardiotonic effects, which may help to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant's berries, leaves, and flowers are used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which may provide health benefits including reduced cholesterol levels, improved digestion, and antioxidant effects. Regular consumption of the Bohemian hawthorn may also help to reduce the risk of heart disease and improve overall well-being.
